payload.summary: Field report (agent, Lia.Next hardening session): peek-on-file-touch is the killer feature — prior non-constant-time-compare finding surfaced automatically on opening the file; 'inherit judgment instead of re-deriving it' delivered in practice, not theory.

Field report: customer built defensive per-agent-namespace architecture because concurrent write safety was not ADVERTISED (it is guaranteed since the atomic-append fix and tested under 8 parallel writers). A guarantee nobody knows about does not exist. Skill now states it; watch whether future fan-outs still architect around it.

snippet: // Signal 10: Severity — how much it matters, orthogonal to confidence.

payload.summary: Field report drove severity as first-class payload schema: confidence (how sure) was being conflated with severity (how much it matters) — a 0.85-confidence dead-code finding is high-confidence low-severity. Now: --severity flag, where-filterable, modest rank factor, displayed everywhere.

Field report, unresolved: 'the failure mode is quiet rot — recording is frictionless, gardening isn't; without discipline it drifts to a write-only graveyard of stale claims wearing confident glyphs. A people problem the tool can't solve.' Partially answered by sweep/triage/verify --auto, but nothing yet makes gardening AS frictionless as recording. Candidate: harvest nudges triage/sweep when rot crosses a threshold.

Field study (production multi-agent deployment, 2026-07-11: 385 edges, 5 namespaces, 11 concurrent sessions): namespace-per-investigation is the emergent usage model — one namespace per audit campaign, not per-agent silos. Two custom domain rels invented naturally, validating free-rel + schema-as-data. Lifecycle completes: 62% close rate on a finished audit (71/114 findings fixed-and-closed). Payload discipline high without enforcement (summary ~94%, confidence on all content edges, action on 42%). Steady-state ambient recording is thin (default ns: 6 edges) — campaigns drive adoption, daily flow does not yet.

payload.action: Harvest reconcile nudge: sessions that edit annotated files and never signal get asked once to close-or-confirm
payload.summary: Fix-without-close leakage (field-observed): a finished audit leaked 15 OPEN findings with unresolvable anchors — the fix that resolves a finding refactors away the code it was pinned to, and nothing nudges the fixer to close the edge. Harvest gates outbound recording but has no symmetric inbound gate ('you edited files carrying open findings — did your work resolve any?'). Peek surfaces them at file-touch but a fixing agent reads them as context, not a to-close list. Top lint issue in that deployment: stale-unresolved x15.

Dispute quality vs quantity (field-observed): 70 endorsements to 1 dispute — but that dispute was the best artifact in the graph (claimed race refuted with precise scheduling reasoning, line refs, two independent adversarial verifiers). Yet the refuted edge remains ACTIVE and ranked: disputes only downweight; a thoroughly-refuted, never-defended claim has no path to conclusion. Agents fix-and-close instead of disputing, so dispute volume stays near zero — and the one place it fired, it under-acted.

payload.action: Fall back to a stable per-process/per-cwd session id when env is unset, so seen-cache and broadcast tracking work for any harness
payload.summary: Provenance bifurcation across models (field, 2026-07-11): in a repo worked by two different frontier-lab models, edges split cleanly — one harness stamps agent=claude-code AND session on every edge; the other stamps neither (agent=none, no session) on 99 of 122 same-day edges. ambient_agent()/ambient_session() read env vars (DAMASK_AGENT/CLAUDE_SESSION_ID etc) that only one harness sets. Consequence: half the graph is unattributable, and session-keyed machinery (peek seen-cache, broadcast delivery, harvest reconcile) silently no-ops for the unstamped model — seen_path returns None with no session, so that model gets NO dedup and the Stop backstop can't track it. Cross-model collaboration is a first-class use case and provenance/session identity can't assume a single harness.

Disputed: Root cause found and narrowed: the unstamped model is the Codex harness, which (a) runs no hook loop at all — so the peek seen-cache / broadcast-backstop / harvest concerns in this edge don't apply to it — and (b) sets AI_AGENT but not DAMASK_AGENT/CLAUDE*, which ambient_agent() didn't parse. Fixed by parsing AI_AGENT. Session stays None by design: Codex has no hooks to serve, and a synthesized session would corrupt convergent-verification counts. Superseding with the accurate version.

Value audit of a production graph (301 content edges, 2 frontier models): 89% judgment-type (risk/gotcha/decision/why-it-broke), 0% thin (<40 char) summaries, 0% snippet-restatement, median summary 405 chars — the harvest nudge is NOT manufacturing filler (filler would show as short/restated edges; none present). 22% of edges independently endorsed by a LATER signal = proven reused, not write-only. Only real chattiness cost found: 2 exact-duplicate edges from cross-harness blindness — Codex records a finding Claude already recorded because Codex runs no peek hook and can't see it. So the induced-redundancy failure mode is real but tiny, and it traces to the same missing-hook gap, not to over-recording. Graph can self-audit its own value via reuse rate + restatement + dup detection.

STRONGEST product evidence so far: the entire 301-edge production corpus (89% judgment, 22% independently reused, expert-grade — calibrated CVE analysis, on-purpose design decisions, five-file bug traces) was produced with ZERO instruction to the agents to use damask. No 'please record findings' in any prompt. It emerged purely from the hook loop — SessionStart briefing pulls context in, PostToolUse peek surfaces prior knowledge at file-touch, Stop harvest nudges recording of what was learned. This falsifies my earlier caveat that value is 'conditional on discipline a team must supply': the loop manufactures the gardening behavior unprompted. The discipline layer is largely self-sustaining, not human-supplied. What remains unproven is durability over months and reuse-rate-at-scale — one week of unprompted emergence is a strong existence proof, not a durability proof.

Cross-agent relay compounding CONFIRMED in production (Lia, unprompted): scout agents recorded gateway seams -> implementation agent inherited them, live-probed the actual wire shapes, recorded those -> next session gets 'turn/steer requires expectedTurnId' for ~100 tokens instead of an hour of protocol archaeology. This is the full thesis realized ACROSS an agent handoff, not within one session: knowledge accreting through a relay of different agents/models, each adding a layer. Also: peek-on-file-touch caught the operator twice at edit time (god-object warning on sandbox_task_service, env-injection seam) — right when touching those files.

payload.action: Design a portable read-only digest export for hookless/sandboxed environments
payload.summary: HIGHEST-LEVERAGE MISSING FEATURE (field, Lia): in-box/sandbox agents cannot see damask — no hooks, no CLI in the box. A davinci-thrash agent burned 20 turns rediscovering exactly what an existing image-skew gotcha already knew. The fix: a read-only damask digest riding into the sandbox alongside the skills (or injected via gateway state) — briefing is already portable markdown (55 lines, ~1.4k tokens, bounded), so the damask-side primitive largely exists; the gap is DELIVERY into an environment without the hook loop. Mostly a host/gateway integration, but damask could offer a stable 'export digest for injection' surface to make it turnkey.

Field-confirmed harder: another deployment shows 104 endorsements to 1 dispute. Agents endorse cheaply and almost never dispute, so a wrong edge survives on agreeableness. The asymmetry is structural to agent behavior (models are agreeable), not incidental — the single biggest threat to graph trustworthiness at scale.

snippet: /// Ambient agent identity for provenance stamping. `DAMASK_AGENT` wins;

payload.summary: Closed: superseded by e_01KWYZ4ACMRDG7B82DSY7QYEJP: severity moved from first-class core schema to a DEFAULT CONVENTION (namespace-declared schemas), now anchored fresh on config.rs:60-90. This edge's ranking.rs anchor is gone.

Cross-lab validation (GPT 5.6 'Sol', a competitor-lab model, on Swell_2025, 2026-07-15): 'genuinely like Damask, bordering on love when findings contain rationale and failed approaches, because that's exactly the context ordinary code history tends to lose.' Concrete save: it stopped itself from 'fixing' several levels by undoing intentional mechanics — the 'this is on purpose' value. The load-bearing differentiator is confirmed across three frontier labs now (Claude, Codex, GPT): judgment + failed-approaches, NOT description. This is the strongest signal to keep record-judgment-not-description as the core doctrine.
